rows:
C2.1 Buildings, including carports, porches, balconies, verandahs, or equivalent, set
back from the primary and secondary street boundaries:
i. a minimum of 3 metres;
ii. reduced by up to 50% (1.5m) provided that the area of any building,
including a garage encroaching into the setback area, is compensated for by
at least an equal area of open space that is located between the street
setback line and a line drawn parallel to it at twice the setback distance
(refer Figure 2a and 2c of the R-Codes)
C2.2 Refer to C2.1 above.
C2.3 Buildings set back from the corner truncation boundary in accordance with C2.1
above.
C2.4 Refer to C2.1 above.

C3.1 Buildings which are set back in accordance with the following provisions, subject
to any additional measures in other elements of the R-Codes:
i. Lot 221 southern boundary: 3.5m minimum to the lower and upper floors
ii. Lot 237 northern boundary: 3.5m minimum to the lower and upper floors
iii. Lot 237 southern boundary: nil at ground floor and 1.5 minimum to the
upper floor
iv. Lot 237: Garage to be set back 1m from Dorre Lane
C3.2 Refer to C3.1 above.

C4 Dwellings to provide 40% open space. Otherwise, open space is provided in
accordance with Table B of the R-Codes.

C6 Building heights which comply with the following:
i. R20: 5.5m maximum external wall height. 8.5m maximum height to roof
ridge
ii. R30: 6m maximum external wall height. 9m maximum height to roof ridge

C4.1 Front fences within the primary street setback area that do not exceed 1.2m
above natural ground level, measured from the primary street side of the front
fence and are at least 50% visually permeable.
C4.2 Solid pillars that form part of front fences not more than 1.2m above natural
ground level provided the horizontal dimension of the pillars is not greater than
400mm by 400mm and pillars are separated by visually permeable fencing in line
with C4.1.

Local Planning Policy - Design
Guideline S6 - South Beach Village
